title: About the Occupational Health and Safety Program - Canada.ca
content:
## About the Occupational Health and Safety Program
This program seeks to promote and sustain safe workplaces in:
- the federally regulated private sector
- the federal public service and Parliamentary precinct
- First Nation Band Councils
The program aims to ensure federal employers comply with relevant occupational health and safety standards through employer and employee cooperation. It helps to:
- protect workers' health and safety in targeted high-risk industries
- provide income support and rehabilitation support to injured federal workers and merchant seamen
Workplaces covered by Part II (Occupational Health and Safety) of the Canada Labour Code : Federally regulated workplaces
- Employers: Preventing harassment and violence in the workplace
- Health and safety in federally regulated workplaces
- Health and Safety Committees and Representatives
- Occupational Health and Safety Advisory Committee

- Reports and publications
- Apply to the Registry of Workplace Harassment and Violence Prevention Investigators Closed

- Employers: Resolve workplace harassment and violence
- Federal Workers' Compensation Service
- Closed: Consultation on providing menstrual products in federally regulated workplaces
- Closed: Consultation regarding mental health (psychological health and safety)
